data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Exits Its Arm Stake, but Not the Technology
data.description:
Shares of British chip designer Arm (ARM) are up on Wednesday after new filings showed that chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) has fully exited its stake in the company. Notably, at the end of the third quarter, Nvidia owned about 1.1 million Arm shares worth roughly $155.8 million, but a Tuesday SEC filing confirmed that the position had been sold. Originally, Nvidia was part of a group of strategic investors that collectively bought $735 million worth of Arm shares during its 2023 Nasdaq debut.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That IPO investment followed Nvidia's failed attempt to acquire Arm outright for $40 billion in 2020 from SoftBank (SFTBF). In fact, the deal collapsed in 2022 after facing regulatory opposition in both the U.S. and Europe. Despite selling its equity stake, Nvidia's relationship with Arm remains intact. After the takeover fell through, Nvidia retained its 20-year Arm license, with CEO Jensen Huang stating that the company would remain a long-term partner. Indeed, Nvidia's Grace CPUs, which it positions as a foundation for next-generation data centers, are built using Arm's chip architecture.

At the same time, Arm is continuing to grow on its own. In its latest quarter, revenue increased by 26% from a year ago to $1.24 billion, which was higher than analysts expected. Interestingly, Morgan Stanley (MS) said the results showed that there is strong momentum tied to AI demand, and that the company is spending more money to support future growth. While the stock slipped slightly after earnings because of cautious guidance, shares are still up 16% this year, and Morgan Stanley kept a Buy rating with a $135 price target.

Is ARM Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on ARM stock based on 18 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average ARM price target of $159.50 per share implies 23.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Ethereum Price Is Acting Like a Magnet for Institutional Giants as Big Money Eyes a $2,500 Rebound
data.description:
Ethereum (ETH-USD) is currently hovering near $1,974, still struggling to push past the major $2,500 mark. Even though the price fell as low as $1,744 earlier this month, the bigwigs of the financial world are not running away. Instead, they are positioning themselves for a comeback. From university endowments to the world's largest fund managers, the trend is that institutional money is looking at Ethereum's utility and staking rewards as a massive opportunity for 2026.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Elite University Funds Shift toward ETH

The Harvard Management Company, which looks after Harvard's $57 billion endowment, recently made a big move. New SEC filings show they bought $87 million worth of BlackRock's Ether ETF. During the same period, they cut their Bitcoin holdings by about 21%. This shift suggests that the smartest money on Wall Street is starting to see more value in the Ethereum ecosystem compared to the digital gold narrative of Bitcoin (BTC-USD).

BlackRock Adds a Bonus to its New ETF

BlackRock (BLK) is upgrading its Ethereum offerings to include staking. Unlike a standard ETF that just tracks the price, this new version will actually earn rewards from the network. BlackRock recently clarified that they will keep an 18% fee from these rewards to pay for the tech and security, while investors get the other 82%. This change makes it much easier for regular people to earn interest on their crypto without managing the technical side themselves.

Big Banks Choose Security over Speed

Ethereum has become the undisputed king of Real-World Assets (RWA). Traditional assets worth over $15 billion, including gold, tokenized stocks, and U.S. Treasurys, now sit on the Ethereum blockchain.

This represents a 200% increase from last year. While other networks like Solana are faster, giants like JPMorgan (JPM) and BlackRock prefer Ethereum because it is the most secure. This massive interest from traditional finance creates a constant demand for ETH that could eventually push the price back toward the $2,500 level.

At the time of writing, Ether is sitting at $1,976.85.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Canada's Restaurant Brands International (QSR) Turns to Promotions to Boost Flagging Sales
data.description:
Canada's Restaurant Brands International (QSR), the parent company of Burger King and Tim Hortons, is turning to promotions to attract customers and boost its flagging sales.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Toronto-based company announced that it is bringing back a popular sweepstakes promotion at its Tim Hortons coffee and doughnut chain called "Roll Up The Rim To Win." The contest allows customers to win items ranging from a free coffee to a new car, and has proven popular in the past.

Tim Hortons made a significant change to the contest in 2020 during the COVID-19 pandemic, switching to a new digital sweepstakes format that proved unpopular with consumers. But now, in an effort to boost sales, Restaurant Brands is bringing back the original contest.

"We're thrilled to share that Rolling Up The Rim of our cups is coming back – starting on Feb. 23 – and not just for this year, but for years to come," said the company in a news release.

Restaurant Brands' Latest Print

This year's contest features more than 30 million prizes available to be won, including vacations, gift cards, electronics, and 17 million coffees. The contest kicks off on Feb. 23 and runs through March 22, according to a Tim Hortons news release.

A week ago, Restaurant Brands reported its latest financial results. While the company posted top and bottom line beats, its Tim Hortons unit was weak during the quarter. Tim Hortons posted same-store sales growth of 2.9%, below the 3.8% anticipated by analysts. Tim Hortons accounts for 46% of Restaurant Brands' annual revenue and is strategically important to the company.

Is QSR Stock a Buy?

Restaurant Brands International's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy, seven Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average QSR price target of $77.06 implies 11.66% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Google Stock Prepares to Fire as it Debuts $499 Pixel 10a to Snuff Out Apple's iPhone 17e
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) shares moved slightly to $303.34 on Wednesday as the company revealed its new Pixel 10a. Launching the phone now allows Google to try to lock in budget buyers before Apple (AAPL) can release its own affordable model, the iPhone 17e. Google's plan is to keep the price at $499, which is $100 cheaper than what Apple is expected to charge. Preorders are open now, and the phones will start shipping on March 5.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Google Chooses AI Smarts over New Hardware

Google is not making big changes to how the phone looks, but it is making the software much smarter. The company added two high-end AI camera tools, Auto Best Take and Camera Coach, to this cheaper model. These features help fix group photos where someone blinked and give live tips on how to take a better picture. The phone also includes Satellite SOS for emergencies. While the screen is a bit brighter than last year, Google kept most of the hardware the same to make sure the price stayed low.

The most unique part of the Pixel 10a is its back. Unlike most modern phones, the camera does not stick out at all. This means the phone can lay perfectly flat on a table without wobbling. Maintaining the cost at $499 required Google to remove the "PixelSnap" magnetic charging found on its more expensive phones.

To sum up, Google is trying to pull a fast one on Apple. Releasing the Pixel 10a first with a lower price serves as a way to tempt people to switch. The phone isn't a massive upgrade in terms of raw power, but it has enough cool AI tricks to feel modern.

Is Google a Good Stock to Buy?

Alphabet's stock GOOGL continues to carry a Strong Buy consensus, based on 32 analyst ratings over the past three months. Out of those, 25 call it a Buy, while seven recommend a Hold. None of the analysts currently suggest selling.

The average 12-month GOOGL price target sits at $379.47, which represents 25% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: US Industrial Production Ramps to 11-Month High as Trade Uncertainty Eases
data.description:
U.S. industrial production rose by 0.7% month-over-month in January, marking the highest rate of growth since February 2025, according to a new report from the Fed. Manufacturing activity, which accounts for roughly 75% of industrial production, increased by 0.6%, also the highest since February 2025.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bringing production back to the U.S. is a key goal of the Trump administration. With trade uncertainty easing and accommodative tax policies, manufacturers, both domestic and foreign, are increasingly investing in U.S. factories.

Industrial Production Gains Momentum

Furthermore, factories are becoming more efficient. During the month, capacity utilization was 75.6%, the highest since September and improving from 75.2% during the previous month. Total industrial utilization was 76.2%, registering a four-month high.

Manufacturing activity could continue to gain. Earlier this month, Institute for Supply Management's manufacturing index for January came in at 52.6, indicating expansion, while manufacturing employment increased for the first time since late 2024.

data.title: PANW Stock Sinks, CEO Says AI Won't Replace Cybersecurity 'Anytime Soon'
data.description:
Palo Alto Networks (PANW) CEO Nikesh Arora pushed back against the idea that artificial intelligence poses an immediate threat to cybersecurity. Speaking on the company's latest earnings call, he said that he does not see AI replacing cybersecurity "anytime soon." In fact, he noted that while he cannot speak for all software, customers are increasingly focused on making their security systems more consistent so they can respond faster with the help of AI. In his view, AI is becoming a tool that strengthens cybersecurity, not one that eliminates it.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Still, the market reaction has been negative. Shares are down at the time of writing after Palo Alto reported second-quarter results that beat Wall Street expectations, though its third-quarter earnings guidance came in lighter than analysts had hoped. More broadly, software stocks have been under pressure as new AI tools from companies like Anthropic and OpenAI raise concerns about long-term disruption. So far this year, the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) has dropped more than 23%, while Palo Alto is down 11% year-to-date and 21% over the past year.

At the same time, Palo Alto has been pushing deeper into AI rather than pulling back. The company introduced a suite of new agentic AI tools in the fourth quarter and has been actively acquiring businesses to expand its cybersecurity capabilities. Earlier this month, it completed its $25 billion acquisition of CyberArk, after finalizing its purchase of Chronosphere in January. It also announced a deal to acquire Israeli cybersecurity startup Koi.

Is PANW Stock a Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on PANW stock based on 28 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average PANW price target of $216.02 implies 40.8% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Eli Lilly Stock (LLY) in Focus as Zepbound Found to Help Treat Psoriasis
data.description:
Eli Lilly's (LLY) stock is back in focus on reports that its weight-loss drug Zepbound can help to treat skin conditions such as psoriasis.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A late-stage study has concluded that when Zepbound is used in conjunction with psoriasis treatment Taltz, patients experienced better outcomes with the skin disease. Those patients afflicted with psoriasis also managed to lose significant amounts of weight.

The ​combination of Zepbound and Taltz showed greater improvement in skin symptoms and weight loss in the study of 274 patients. ⁠About ‌27% of patients who received Taltz ⁠and Zepbound reached complete skin clearance and at least 10% weight loss, compared to 5.8% of patients treated with Taltz alone. Psoriasis is a chronic condition that causes scaly patches ​on the skin.

The Everything Drug?

Nearly all trial participants had psoriasis affecting sensitive areas such as the face, scalp or genitals, which are difficult to ⁠treat, said Eli Lilly in a news release. In the U.S., about 61% of people ‌with psoriasis also have obesity or ‌are overweight with at least one weight-related co-morbidity.

The latest finding that Zepbound could help treat certain skin conditions bolsters the case that its Zepbound GLP-1 medication could become an everything drug, or as some analysts term it, a "miracle drug." Zepbound and other GLP-1 medications have been found to help with high blood pressure, heart disease, and even addictions such as cigarette use and alcohol consumption.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,258.71 implies 22.43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: XRP Retail Traders Are Getting Scared and Selling, While Bigwigs Are Buying the Dip on XRP's Price
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) recently bounced back to $1.46 after falling to a low of $1.12 this month. Even though the price is lower than last year's peak, the market shows signs that the worst is over. Many regular traders got scared and sold, but big investors are doing the opposite. They are treating this $1.12 level like a solid floor. Instead of worrying about more drops, these big players are focused on how quickly the price might climb back toward $2.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Whales Hide their Coins in Private Vaults

The amount of XRP available to buy on exchanges has dropped to its lowest point in five years. Right now, only 12.9 billion XRP is sitting on exchanges, which is the lowest since 2021. On Binance, the supply fell to just 2.57 billion coins. This means investors are moving their money into private wallets where they can't sell it quickly. When there are fewer coins for sale on exchanges, even a small amount of buying can cause the price to jump high very fast.

Sellers Pay a Fee Because They Are Too Pessimistic

Traders betting that XRP will go down are currently in a tough spot. On the Binance exchange, the fee to bet against XRP reached its lowest level since early 2025. This means there are so many people betting on a crash that they actually have to pay the bulls just to keep their trades open. Usually, when everyone is this negative at the same time, it leads to a short squeeze. This is when the price goes up a little bit, and those sellers are forced to buy back their coins, which pushes the price even higher.

Big Banks and Funds Keep Buying the Dip

Even when the price was crashing, professional investors kept putting money into XRP ETFs. These funds have seen new money come in on 53 of the last 59 days. They now hold over $1 billion in assets. This is different from regular people trading on an app; these are big institutions with long-term plans. Their steady buying helps keep the price from falling too far and shows they still believe the coin has a lot of value.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.4662.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: U.S. Housing Starts Leap to 5-Month High as Affordability Concerns Persist
data.description:
Homebuilders, like Lennar (LEN) and D.R. Horton (DHI), are back in business. In December, housing starts increased by 6.2%, the highest level in five months, to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 1.4 million homes. Single-family housing starts, which account for the majority of homebuilding, rose by 4.1% to an annualized rate of 981,000 homes.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In another positive sign for the homebuilding industry, building permits, which signal future construction activity, grew by 4.3% to an annualized rate of 1.45 million, registering the highest level since March.

Builder Confidence Slips as Buyers Remain Hesitant

Improving housing market conditions were likely behind the rise in both housing starts and building permits. Homebuilder sentiment ticked higher as 2025 came to a close, although confidence has since slipped as concerns mount over affordability.

"While the majority of builders continue to deploy buyer incentives, including price cuts, many prospective buyers remain on the sidelines," said National Association of Home Builders Chairman Buddy Hughes. Furthermore, buyers must still grapple with mortgage rates above 6%, while builders face elevated construction costs.
